module gg
    contains
    function func_arr3() result(ret_arr)
        integer,allocatable :: ret_arr(:)
        print *, allocated(ret_arr)
        allocate(ret_arr(1))
        ret_arr = [911]
        ! print *, "worked", inp_ref
    end function
end module


program p
    use gg
    implicit none
    integer, allocatable :: inp(:)
    integer :: i =77
    
    do i= 1, 3
        inp = func_arr3()
    end do 
end program  
```

F
T
T
assem@assem-PC:~/Desktop/fortran/compile_gsnap$ lfortran tt6.f90 --experimental-simplifier
F
assem@assem-PC:~/Desktop/fortran/compile_gsnap$ gfortran tt6.f90  && ./a.out 
 F
 F
 F

```

#2257 (https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2257#issuecomment-2041693935) + towards finalization
This handles the descriptorStrings `(character(:), alloctable :: str)` to transform the following code :
```fortran 
program p
    print *, func_str()    

    contains 

    function func_str() result(ret_str)
        character(:), allocatable :: ret_str
    end function
end program  
```
to 
```fortran
! Fortran code after applying the pass: insert_deallocate
program p
implicit none
character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable :: __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
call func_str(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res)
print *, __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
deallocate(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res) ! Implicit deallocate

contains

subroutine func_str(ret_str)
    character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable, intent(out) :: ret_str
end subroutine func_str

end program p
```

I think we should do the same with normal strings (pointerString) `character(10) :: str` as it's being heap allocated so it needs to have proper owner then later in `insert_deallocate` pass we can properly deallocate #2257 (https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2257#issuecomment-2041693935) + towards finalization
This handles the descriptorStrings `(character(:), alloctable :: str)` to transform the following code :
```fortran 
program p
    print *, func_str()    

    contains 

    function func_str() result(ret_str)
        character(:), allocatable :: ret_str
    end function
end program  
```
to 
```fortran
! Fortran code after applying the pass: insert_deallocate
program p
implicit none
character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable :: __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
call func_str(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res)
print *, __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
deallocate(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res) ! Implicit deallocate

contains

subroutine func_str(ret_str)
    character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable, intent(out) :: ret_str
end subroutine func_str

end program p
```

I think we should do the same with normal strings (pointerString) `character(10) :: str` as it's being heap allocated so it needs to have proper owner then later in `insert_deallocate` pass we can properly deallocate owner)","user":{"login":"assem2002","id":75497299,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc1NDk3Mjk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/75497299?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002","html_url":"https://github.com/assem2002","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Towards #2257 (https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2257#issuecomment-2041693935) + towards finalization
This handles the descriptorStrings `(character(:), alloctable :: str)` to transform the following code :
```fortran 
program p
    print *, func_str()    

    contains 

    function func_str() result(ret_str)
        character(:), allocatable :: ret_str
    end function
end program  
```
to 
```fortran
! Fortran code after applying the pass: insert_deallocate
program p
implicit none
character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable :: __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
call func_str(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res)
print *, __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
deallocate(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res) ! Implicit deallocate

contains

subroutine func_str(ret_str)
    character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable, intent(out) :: ret_str
end subroutine func_str

end program p
```

I think we should do the same with normal strings (pointerString) `character(10) :: str` as it's being heap allocated so it needs to have proper owner then later in `insert_deallocate` pass we can properly deallocate #2257 (https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/2257#issuecomment-2041693935) + towards finalization
This handles the descriptorStrings `(character(:), alloctable :: str)` to transform the following code :
```fortran 
program p
    print *, func_str()    

    contains 

    function func_str() result(ret_str)
        character(:), allocatable :: ret_str
    end function
end program  
```
to 
```fortran
! Fortran code after applying the pass: insert_deallocate
program p
implicit none
character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable :: __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
call func_str(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res)
print *, __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
deallocate(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res) ! Implicit deallocate

contains

subroutine func_str(ret_str)
    character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable, intent(out) :: ret_str
end subroutine func_str

end program p
```

I think we should do the same with normal strings (pointerString) `character(10) :: str` as it's being heap allocated so it needs to have proper owner then later in `insert_deallocate` pass we can properly deallocate ; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@subroutines_12.nang = internal global i64 2

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @my_subroutine()
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

define void @my_subroutine() {
.entry:

```console
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

@subroutines_12.nang = internal global i64 2

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  call void @my_subroutine()
  call void @_lpython_free_argv()
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

define void @my_subroutine() {
.entry:
  %0 = load i64, i64* @subroutines_12.nang, align 4
  %1 = mul i32 1, i64 %0
  %2 = mul i32 %1, 4
  %3 = call i8* @_lfortran_malloc(i32 %2)
  %4 = bitcast i8* %3 to i32*
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  call void @_lfortran_free(i8* %3)
  ret void
}

declare i8* @_lfortran_malloc(i32)

declare void @_lfortran_free(i8*)

library"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-12-13T08:59:23Z","updated_at":"2024-12-15T06:59:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"- **ArrayPhysicalType `PointerToDataArray`** happens to be just a pointer to memory with no descriptors or any other information. The issue is that it uses `lfortran_malloc` function which accepts argument of type `i32` so the allocation would result in unknown behavior.
 
- **ArrayPhysicalType `DescriptorArray`** corresponds to array_descriptor with this description `%dimension_descriptor = type { i32, i32, i32 }`; If it happens that we want to allocate `i64` dimension, that would result in unknown behavior as the size would be messed up and it would be a negative value + the call to `lfortran_malloc` will receive a negative value due to the wrong parameter that the function has (`i32`) .

```
  integer,allocatable:: x(:)
  x = [1,2,3,4]
  print "(*(1X,I0))",x
  x = [5,6]
  print "(*(1X,I0))",x
end program
```
Lfortran 0.42.0 output:
```
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```
Four other compilers gave this output, which IMHO is correct:
```
 1 2 3 4
 5 6
```

Minimal Reproducible Example

```fortran
program main

   implicit none

   character(len=2), allocatable :: line
   character(len=2) :: c

   allocate(character(len=2) :: line)
   line = "ab"

   read(line, *) c
   
   print *, c

end program main
```

``` Fortran
program progg
    character(20) :: i(2)
    character(20) :: str
    str = "LFortran"

    i(1)  = str
    print *, i(1)

    str = "whatever"
    print *, str
    print* ,i(1)
end program progg
```

```Console
assem@assem-PC:~/Desktop/fortran/compile_gsnap$ lfortran tt6.f90 
LFortran            
whatever            
whatever            
assem@assem-PC:~/Desktop/fortran/compile_gsnap$ gfortran tt6.f90 && ./a.out 
 LFortran            
 whatever            
 LFortran            

```

```fortran
module modd
    interface oo
    procedure :: ff
    end interface oo
    contains 
    function ff(rr) result(r)
        integer, allocatable :: r(:)
        integer :: rr(:)
    end function
end module modd

program test
    use modd
    
    type :: m
    end type m
    integer, allocatable :: ll(:)

    type(m) :: tt2
    contains
    subroutine ffo(dummy) 
        class(m):: dummy
        integer, allocatable :: r(:)
        integer, dimension(5) :: rr
        r = oo(rr)
    end subroutine ffo
end program test
```
```console
ASR verify pass error: ExternalSymbol::m_original_name ('ff') + scope_names not found in a module 'modd'
Internal Compiler Error: Unhandled exception
Traceback (most recent call last):
  Binary file "/home/assem/lfortran_test/lfortran/src/bin/lfortran", in _start
  File "./csu/../csu/libc-start.c", line 392, in __libc_start_main
  File "./csu/../sysdeps/nptl/libc_start_call_main.h", line 58, in __libc_start_call_main
  File "/home/assem/lfortran_test/lfortran/src/bin/lfortran.cpp", line 2729, in main
    LCompilers::print_stack_on_segfault();
  File "/home/assem/lfortran_test/lfortran/src/bin/lfortran.cpp", line 2521, in main_app(int, char**)
    }
  File "/home/assem/lfortran_test/lfortran/src/bin/lfortran.cpp", line 822, in (anonymous namespace)::dump_all_passes(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, LCompilers::CompilerOptions&)

If we change `class` into --> `type`, it works. That's due to some externalSymbol that gets created when we use `class` instead of `type`. after `pass_by_array` run, it changes function `ff` name into another name which raises that error we have.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5844/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5844/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

```fortran
module modd
    interface oo
    procedure :: ff
    end interface oo
    contains 
    function ff(rr) result(r)
        integer, allocatable :: r(:)
        integer :: rr(:)
    end function
end module modd

program test
    use modd
    
    type :: m
    end type m
    integer, allocatable :: ll(:)

    type(m) :: tt2
    contains
    subroutine ffo(dummy) 
        class(m):: dummy
        integer, allocatable :: r(:)
        integer, dimension(5) :: rr
        r = oo(rr)
    end subroutine ffo
end program test
```

working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-04T19:11:00Z","updated_at":"2024-11-04T19:11:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MRE
```Fortran
module B
   type tt
      character(:), allocatable :: ssss
   end type tt

   contains

   subroutine sub1(str_tt)
      type(tt), intent(out) :: str_tt
      print *,allocated(str_tt%ssss)
   end subroutine

   subroutine sub2(str)
      character(:), allocatable, intent(out) :: str
      print *, allocated(str)
   end subroutine

end module

program a 
   use B
   type(tt) :: s1
   character(:), allocatable :: allocatable_str
   allocate(character(10) :: allocatable_str)
   allocate( character(10) :: s1%ssss)
   call sub1(s1)
   call sub2(allocatable_str)
end program 
```
GFortran Outputs :
```
F
F
```
LFortran Outputs : 
```
T
F
```

That's because of the `implicitDeallocate` node isn't inserted for the `type(tt)` passed argument.
ASR Snippet:
```Clj
(Allocate
        [((StructInstanceMember
            (Var 6 s1)
            6 1_tt_ssss
            (Allocatable
                (Character 1 -2 () DescriptorString)
            )
            ()
        )
        []
        (IntegerConstant 10 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        ())]
        ()
        ()
        ()
    )
    (SubroutineCall
        6 sub1
        ()
        [((Var 6 s1))]
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
    )
    (SubroutineCall
        6 sub2
        ()
        [((Var 6 allocatable_str))]
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
    )]
),

The ASR goes from this :
```clj
(Allocate
        [((StructInstanceMember
            (Var 6 s1)
            6 1_tt_ssss
            (Allocatable
                (Character 1 -2 () DescriptorString)
            )
            ()
        )
        []
        (IntegerConstant 10 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        ())]
        ()
        ()
        ()
    )
    (SubroutineCall
        6 sub1
        ()
        [((Var 6 s1))]
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
    )
    (SubroutineCall
        6 sub2
        ()
        [((Var 6 allocatable_str))]
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
    )]
),
```
To this :

```clj
      (Allocate
          [((StructInstanceMember
              (Var 6 s1)
              6 1_tt_ssss
              (Allocatable
                  (Character 1 -2 () DescriptorString)
              )
              ()
          )
          []
          (IntegerConstant 10 (Integer 4) Decimal)
          ())]
          ()
          ()
          ()
      )
      (ImplicitDeallocate
          [(StructInstanceMember
              (Var 6 s1)
              3 ssss
              (Allocatable
                  (Character 1 -2 () DescriptorString)
              )
              ()
          )]
      )
      (SubroutineCall
          6 sub1
          ()
          [((Var 6 s1))]
          ()
      )
      (ImplicitDeallocate
          [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
      )
      (SubroutineCall
          6 sub2
          ()
          [((Var 6 allocatable_str))]
          ()
      )
      (ImplicitDeallocate
          [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
      )]
  ),

#5249 

The ASR goes from this :
```clj
(Allocate
        [((StructInstanceMember
            (Var 6 s1)
            6 1_tt_ssss
            (Allocatable
                (Character 1 -2 () DescriptorString)
            )
            ()
        )
        []
        (IntegerConstant 10 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        ())]
        ()
        ()
        ()
    )
    (SubroutineCall
        6 sub1
        ()
        [((Var 6 s1))]
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
    )
    (SubroutineCall
        6 sub2
        ()
        [((Var 6 allocatable_str))]
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
    )]
),
```
To this :

```clj
(Allocate
        [((StructInstanceMember
            (Var 6 s1)
            6 1_tt_ssss
            (Allocatable
                (Character 1 -2 () DescriptorString)
            )
            ()
        )
        []
        (IntegerConstant 10 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        ())]
        ()
        ()
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(StructInstanceMember
            (Var 6 s1)
            6 1_tt_ssss
            (Allocatable
                (Character 1 -2 () DescriptorString)
            )
            ()
        )]
    )
    (SubroutineCall
        6 sub1
        ()
        [((Var 6 s1))]
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
    )
    (SubroutineCall
        6 sub2
        ()
        [((Var 6 allocatable_str))]
        ()
    )
    (ImplicitDeallocate
        [(Var 6 allocatable_str)]
    )]
),


```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5264/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5264/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2463010938","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5264#issuecomment-2463010938","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5264","id":2463010938,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6SzpB6","user":{"login":"assem2002","id":75497299,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc1NDk3Mjk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/75497299?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002","html_url":"https://github.com/assem2002","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-07T19:05:49Z","updated_at":"2024-11-07T19:05:49Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"@certik The PR now passes tests.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2463010938/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","number":5373,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/5373","id":2176269734,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCpMuHc6Btz2m","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5373","diff_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5373.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5373.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5373","number":5373,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Initalize save variables in program for arrays and complex","user":{"login":"assem2002","id":75497299,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc1NDk3Mjk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/75497299?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002","html_url":"https://github.com/assem2002","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Towards #5319.
The fix doesn't work for structs yet. but it should be easy after this PR to do so.

I don't like the workarounds in `init_expr` and `simplifier` pass to make this feature work. They simplify things for the backend, but that's the way I found it would work.

The fix doesn't work for structs yet. but it should be easy after this PR to do so.

I don't like the workarounds in `init_expr` and `simplifier` pass to make this feature work. They simplify things for the backend, but that's the way I found it would work.

Things that gets done in `program` instead of locally in the function or while creating the module (like allocating an array desc) are getting a lot. maybe we need to look into that matter to simplify things further. maybe use lambda functions to preserve these actions so it get applied one by one without caring about preserving the arguments and calling the needed function. #4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

### TODO:

```fortran 
program arrays_57

real :: A(2,2) = reshape([1.0,2.0,3.0,4.0], [2,2])
call trans(A)
print *, A 

contains

subroutine trans(A)
real, intent(inout) :: A(:, :)
real :: B(size(A,1), size(A,2))  !! This line creates local variable with size same as A 
B = A
A = matprod(transpose(B)) !! Local variable is Passed in transpose
end subroutine

function matprod(x) result(k)
real,intent(in) :: x(:,:)
real :: k(size(x, 1), size(x, 2))
k = 12.91
end function

end program

If it is an ExternalSymbol, how would it add it in? I think the GenericProcedure has to be duplicated. It thought we already do it.

I think we do have this feature. This example works with LFortran :
```Fortran
module inf_mod_1
 implicit none
 private
 public::is_finite
 interface is_finite
     module procedure is_finite_1
 end interface is_finite
 contains
 subroutine is_finite_1() 
    print *, "is_finite_1 called"
 end subroutine is_finite_1
 end module inf_mod_1
 
 module infnan_mod_1
 use, non_intrinsic :: inf_mod_1, only : is_finite
 interface is_finite
    module procedure fff
 end interface
 contains
 subroutine fff(x)
    integer :: x
    print *, x
 end subroutine fff
 end module infnan_mod_1
 
 
 program bobyqa_exmp
    call calfun()
    contains
    subroutine calfun()
       use, non_intrinsic :: infnan_mod_1, only : is_finite
       call is_finite()
       call is_finite(2)
    end subroutine calfun
 end program bobyqa_exmp
```

It changes the ASR for the example `where (xbdi == 0) ssq = max(0, ssq - xbdi)` from 
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Array
        (Integer 4)
        [((IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        (ArraySize
            (Var 3 a)
            ()
            (Integer 4)
            ()
        ))]
        PointerToDataArray
    )
    ()
)
```
To
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Integer 4)  ;; HERE IS THE CHANGE
    ()
)

It changes the ASR for the example `where (xbdi == 0) ssq = max(0, ssq - xbdi)` from 
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Array
        (Integer 4)
        [((IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        (ArraySize
            (Var 3 a)
            ()
            (Integer 4)
            ()
        ))]
        PointerToDataArray
    )
    ()
)
```
To
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Integer 4)  ;; HERE IS THE CHANGE
    ()
)

#5433
It changes the ASR for the example `where (xbdi == 0) ssq = max(0, ssq - xbdi)` from 
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Array
        (Integer 4)
        [((IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        (ArraySize
            (Var 3 a)
            ()
            (Integer 4)
            ()
        ))]
        PointerToDataArray
    )
    ()
)
```
To
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Integer 4)  ;; HERE IS THE CHANGE
    ()
)

module inf_mod_1
  implicit none
  private
  public::is_finite
  interface is_finite
      module procedure is_finite_1
  end interface is_finite
  contains
  subroutine is_finite_1() 
     print *, "is_finite_1 called"
  end subroutine is_finite_1
  end module inf_mod_1
  
  module infnan_mod_1
  use, non_intrinsic :: inf_mod_1
  interface is_finite
     module procedure fff
  end interface
  contains
  subroutine fff(x)
     integer :: x
     print *, x
  end subroutine fff
end module infnan_mod_1


program bobyqa_exmp
  use, non_intrinsic :: infnan_mod_1
  interface is_finite
       procedure fffm
  end interface
  call calfun()
      contains
  subroutine fffm(x)
      real :: x
      print *, x
  end subroutine fffm
  subroutine calfun()
      call is_finite()
      call is_finite(2)
      call is_finite(2.2)
  end subroutine calfun
  end program bobyqa_exmp
```
We import the interface into `program` as `ExternalSymbol`, It should be duplicated and extended while creating the The test provided doesn't work on main because `where`  pass creates a wrong return type for `BinOp` which is `Array` though that `where` pass has done its job on itemizing the arrays, and then `array_op` sees the `BinOp` as `array` expression then it tries to loop on it and for sure I can't see what it tries to do as the final ASR is meaningless.
On the other hand `--experimental-simplifier` doesn't depend on `where` pass to itemize the array so the return type of `BinOp` is correct so all of that mess I mentioned above doesn't occur. #5433
It changes the ASR for the example `where (xbdi == 0) ssq = max(0, ssq - xbdi)` from 
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Array
        (Integer 4)
        [((IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        (ArraySize
            (Var 3 a)
            ()
            (Integer 4)
            ()
        ))]
        PointerToDataArray
    )
    ()
)
```
To
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Integer 4)  ;; HERE IS THE CHANGE
    ()
)

#5433
It changes the ASR for the example `where (xbdi == 0) ssq = max(0, ssq - xbdi)` from 
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Array
        (Integer 4)
        [((IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        (ArraySize
            (Var 3 a)
            ()
            (Integer 4)
            ()
        ))]
        PointerToDataArray
    )
    ()
)
```
To
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Integer 4)  ;; HERE IS THE CHANGE
    ()
)

#5433
It changes the ASR for the example `where (xbdi == 0) ssq = max(0, ssq - xbdi)` from 
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Array
        (Integer 4)
        [((IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        (ArraySize
            (Var 3 a)
            ()
            (Integer 4)
            ()
        ))]
        PointerToDataArray
    )
    ()
)
```
To
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Integer 4)  ;; HERE IS THE CHANGE
    ()
)

#5433
It changes the ASR for the example `where (xbdi == 0) ssq = max(0, ssq - xbdi)` from 
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Array
        (Integer 4)
        [((IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        (ArraySize
            (Var 3 a)
            ()
            (Integer 4)
            ()
        ))]
        PointerToDataArray
    )
    ()
)
```
To
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Integer 4)  ;; HERE IS THE CHANGE
    ()
)

{"action":"closed","number":5450,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/5450","id":2193869258,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCpMuHc6Cw8nK","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5450","diff_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5450.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5450.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5450","number":5450,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Modify return type for `BinOp` after `where` pass itemize the array","user":{"login":"assem2002","id":75497299,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc1NDk3Mjk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/75497299?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002","html_url":"https://github.com/assem2002","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Fixes #5433
It changes the ASR for the example `where (xbdi == 0) ssq = max(0, ssq - xbdi)` from 
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Array
        (Integer 4)
        [((IntegerConstant 1 (Integer 4) Decimal)
        (ArraySize
            (Var 3 a)
            ()
            (Integer 4)
            ()
        ))]
        PointerToDataArray
    )
    ()
)
```
To
```Clojure
(IntegerBinOp
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 ssq)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    Sub
    (ArrayItem
        (Var 3 xbdi)
        [(()
        (Var 3 __1_k)
        ())]
        (Integer 4)
        RowMajor
        ()
    )
    (Integer 4)  ;; HERE IS THE CHANGE
    ()
)

        f = "hi"
    else 
        print *, allocated(f)
        f = f // ", there"
    end if
    print *, f
end subroutine ff
end module a

    integer :: i

    call ff(2)

working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-24T06:41:13Z","updated_at":"2024-11-24T06:41:13Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"```fortran

subroutine test_02(ref)  
    character(:) , allocatable, save :: f
    integer :: ref
    if(ref == 1) then
        print *, allocated(f)
        f = "hi"
    else 
        print *, allocated(f)
        f = f // ", there"
    end if
    print *, f
end subroutine test_02


program pp
 call test_02(1)
 call test_02(-1)
end program
```

This mainly because we store `null` in string `f` in each time we call the function `test_02`","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5473/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5473/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

#4397 

MRE:
```fortran
program test
implicit none

real(4) :: B(3, 3)
integer(4) :: A(3)
A = [3,1,2]
B = transpose(B(:, A))

end program
```

GFortran compiles this, but LFortran gives the error:
```
semantic error: `transpose` accepts arrays of rank 2 only, provided an array with rank, 1
 --> test.f90:7:15
  |
7 | B = transpose(B(:, A))
  |               ^^^^^^^


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

We now free allocatables inside `StructType` like:

```fortran
program main
    type my_type
        integer, allocatable :: int(:)
    end type

    type(my_type) :: x

    allocate(x%int(2))
end program
```

```fortran
program test_complex_printing
complex, dimension(2,2) :: V
V(1,1)=cmplx(4.0,2.0)
print *, V(1,1)
V(1,2)=0.0
print *, V(1,2)
V(2,1)=0.5
print *, V(2,1)
V(2,2)=cmplx(5.0,3.0)
print *, V(2,2)
print *, V
end program
```
This prints:
```console
$ lfortran a.f90 --implicit-typing
4.00000000e+00    2.00000000e+00
0.00000000e+00    0.00000000e+00
5.00000000e-01    0.00000000e+00
5.00000000e+00    3.00000000e+00
4.00000000e+00    2.00000000e+00    5.00000000e-01    0.00000000e+00    4.00000000e+00    2.00000000e+00    5.00000000e-01    0.00000000e+00
$ gfortran a.f90 && ./a.out       
             (4.00000000,2.00000000)
             (0.00000000,0.00000000)
            (0.500000000,0.00000000)
             (5.00000000,3.00000000)
             (4.00000000,2.00000000)            (0.500000000,0.00000000)             (0.00000000,0.00000000)             (5.00000000,3.00000000)
```
The individual components are correct, but the whole array is only correct for the first two elements, the other two are {"action":"opened","number":5512,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/5512","id":2205707931,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCpMuHc6DeG6b","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5512","diff_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5512.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5512.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5512","number":5512,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"enh : Get subroutine_from_function pass to handle strings to make the caller be the owner of the strings return ","user":{"login":"assem2002","id":75497299,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc1NDk3Mjk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/75497299?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002","html_url":"https://github.com/assem2002","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Towards #2257 
This handles the descriptorStrings `(character(:), alloctable :: str)` to transform the following code :
```fortran 
program p
    print *, func_str()    

    contains 

    function func_str() result(ret_str)
        character(:), allocatable :: ret_str
    end function
end program  
```
to 
```fortran
! Fortran code after applying the pass: insert_deallocate
program p
implicit none
character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable :: __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
call func_str(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res)
print *, __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
deallocate(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res) ! Implicit deallocate

contains

subroutine func_str(ret_str)
    character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable, intent(out) :: ret_str
end subroutine func_str

end program p
```

I think we should do the same with normal strings (pointerString) `character(10) :: str` as it's being heap allocated so it needs to have proper owner then later in `insert_deallocate` pass we can #2257 + towards finalization
This handles the descriptorStrings `(character(:), alloctable :: str)` to transform the following code :
```fortran 
program p
    print *, func_str()    

    contains 

    function func_str() result(ret_str)
        character(:), allocatable :: ret_str
    end function
end program  
```
to 
```fortran
! Fortran code after applying the pass: insert_deallocate
program p
implicit none
character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable :: __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
call func_str(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res)
print *, __libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res
deallocate(__libasr__created__var__0__func_call_res) ! Implicit deallocate

contains

subroutine func_str(ret_str)
    character(len=:, kind=1), allocatable, intent(out) :: ret_str
end subroutine func_str

end program p
```

I think we should do the same with normal strings (pointerString) `character(10) :: str` as it's being heap allocated so it needs to have proper owner then later in `insert_deallocate` pass we can properly deallocate {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/comments/1794776253","pull_request_review_id":2359175262,"id":1794776253,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOCpMuHc5q-hy9","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+import subprocess
+
+def is_file_binary(path: str) -> bool:
+    try:
+        with open(path, 'r') as fp:
+            fp.read(16)
+            return False
+    except UnicodeDecodeError:
+            return True
+    
+def get_commit_hashes_PR_against_main() -> list : # returns list of strings
+    completedProcess_object =  subprocess.run(["git" ,"rev-list" ,"origin/main..HEAD"], stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
+    commit_hashes_listed  : list  = completedProcess_object.stdout.split("\n")
+    if(commit_hashes_listed[-1] == ""): commit_hashes_listed.remove("")
+    print("number of commit from current PR branch against origin/main is :", len(commit_hashes_listed))
+    print (completedProcess_object.stdout)
+    return commit_hashes_listed
+
+def get_changed_files() -> list : # returns list of strings.
+    completedProcess_object = subprocess.run(["git","diff","--name-only","origin/main", "--diff-filter=d"], stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)","path":"check_binary_file_in_git_history.py","commit_id":"fd40749b974229008edfa88c12b71539d831668b","original_commit_id":"fd40749b974229008edfa88c12b71539d831668b","user":{"login":"assem2002","id":75497299,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc1NDk3Mjk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/75497299?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002","html_url":"https://github.com/assem2002","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/assem2002/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Okay. So we here just specify the compiler options only to get passed while comliling fortran > Nice, just one failure for LLVM >= 17:
> 
> ```
> LCOMPILERS_ASSERT failed: /home/runner/work/lfortran/lfortran/src/libasr/codegen/llvm_utils.cpp

> type
> ```
> 
> The other tests already pass. It seems a little bit too good to be true that just a few tests have to be skipped, but if this works, then this will be easier than I thought.

I think it should be like that, we don't use the pass at all at the moment.

Description

Because of use of `static` variable, `make_FunctionCall_t_util` always uses the default value set for it, we need to make it an `external` variable instead, that's one solution.

Other solution to the problem could be use to setter and getter for `use_experimental_simplifier` variable.

Other solution to the problem could be use to setter and getter for `use_experimental_simplifier` variable.

- some minor string descriptor pointer loading issues.
- passing and returning a string descriptor in functions.


**Next to do** : 
- Get all tests to pass
